verdict_reasoning: HMGB1 is canonically a nuclear non-histone chromatin protein, but it has a well-documented non-classical secretion pathway. Under inflammatory stimulation, hypoxia, oxidative stress, or cell death (apoptosis, necroptosis, pyroptosis, NETosis), HMGB1 is actively secreted or passively released and then binds cell-surface receptors (RAGE, TLR2, TLR4) — but critically, HMGB1 itself also translocates to the outer leaflet of the plasma membrane in a cell-state-dependent manner, demonstrated by surface biotinylation and flow cytometry on intact (non-permeabilized) cells in cancer and ICD contexts. Several antibody and CAR-T programs have targeted extracellular HMGB1. Surface display is thus real but strictly non-baseline (requires stress, ICD, or activation state), qualifying as contextual / cell_state_induced rather than constitutive.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Whether HMGB1 stably associates with the outer PM leaflet (anchored to a TM partner) or is merely present in the pericellular milieu after release; wash-resistance data specific to surface-bound form are limited.
